model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card

Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the "Beta" project card.
The current crosshair is at approximately (153, 126).
The "Beta" project card is the middle card in the top row.
The center of the Beta card is roughly at (387, 278).

Let's estimate the offset:
dx = 387 - 153 = 234
dy = 278 - 126 = 152

Wait, let me re-examine the image.
Crosshair center: x=153, y=126 (approx)
Beta card:
Left edge: ~270
Right edge: ~504
Top edge: ~183
Bottom edge: ~373
Center of Beta card: x = (270+504)/2 = 387, y = (183+373)/2 = 278

Offset dx = 387 - 153 = 234
Offset dy = 278 - 126 = 152

Let's refine coordinates.
Crosshair: x=153, y=126
Beta card center:

Card 3 (Gamma): x~624, y~278
Card 4 (Delta): x~150, y~487
Card 5 (Epsilon): x~387, y~487
Card 6 (Zeta): x~624, y~487

dx = 387 - 153 = 234
dy = 278 - 126 = 152
